title: Decaneuropsis philippinensis
taxonomy: Decaneuropsis philippinensis is classified within the plant family Celastraceae, commonly known as the bittershrub or staff vine family. This family consists of approximately 90 genera and 1,000 species. Decaneuropsis philippinensis is a species of flowering plant endemic to the Philippines. The plant's classification is further categorized into the order Celastrales and the clade Rosidae. The Celastraceae family is characterized by its woody shrubs or small trees and often produces showy, fragrant flowers.
uses: Decaneuropsis philippinensis, also known as the Philippine violet or Philippine orchid tree, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are used in traditional remedies to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves are also employed to treat coughs and respiratory issues. Furthermore, the plant's roots are believed to have antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Decaneuropsis philippinensis for medicinal purposes.
